enrichment.summary: Kyle records a morning vlog from Boulder, recapping a day without paid work where he helped his mother with cleaning and organizing, then worked on his webpage late into the night. He shares his current audiobook rotation method—sampling books in a day to decide which ones to revisit—and briefly mentions titles he's been listening to.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: accepts cancellations as a downside of his cleaning job without complaint
enthusiastically endorses going through an audiobook in one day to decide if it's worth returning to
finds books packed with real information more valuable than flowery, poetic ones that say nothing

entities: Captain Coucon (likely Captain Courageous or similar)

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.claim: I don't have a YouTube page.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I listened to 'Captivity and Restoration' by Mary Rowlandson from 1680.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I like the method of going through an audiobook in one day to understand its style and content.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Some audiobooks are flowery poetic plays on words and don't mean much.

claims.claim: I might do a book review.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have oatmeal on my chin.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm just working on my webpage; it's all I do.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had oatmeal and fruit for breakfast.

She wanted me to, uh, help her do random things.
Cleaning house and organizing and stuff for her business and stuff.
And then came home and worked until like, uh,
Ten o'clock, I think. So, just regular all day.

Web page that I'm working on.
Um, yeah.
Kind of getting to the point of seeing what it'll look like in the end.
It's exciting.
And then I get going on my YouTube page.
But that's about what's going on with my life.
Listening to, um, yesterday it was the China Treaty by Mark Twain.
And then the day before that it was, um, um,
Captivity and Restoration by Mary...
Her last name.
And King, uh, Phillips War in 1680.
Pretty interesting.
Getting captured by Indians.
And, uh, anyways, yeah, it's exciting, man.
I, I like this method of you go through an audio book in one day.
Just so you know what it's all about.
You know, the kind of style or whatever.
And if you like it or not.
And then you just move on to the next one.
And then you get to sift out the ones that you really gotta get back to.
You know what I mean?
And some are just crazy and interesting.
Like, uh, Captain Cook and...

That was really good.
Some are really good.
